title: India Finalizes Badminton Squad for Asian Games 2026
indian_angle: Indian badminton fans will be keen to know the full roster representing the nation at the prestigious Asian Games.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the final team selection for the upcoming 20th Asian Games in 2026, setting the stage for India's challenge.

title: Tanvi Sharma's Game: Saina or Sindhu Style?
indian_angle: This article offers an intriguing analysis of young Indian talent Tanvi Sharma's playing style, comparing her to two of India's greatest badminton icons.
summary: An analysis delves into the playing style of rising Indian shuttler Tanvi Sharma, drawing comparisons to the tactical approaches of Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.

title: Grassroots Coaches Program Kicks Off in Guwahati and Raipur
indian_angle: This program aims to strengthen the foundation of Indian badminton by developing skilled coaches at the grassroots level in key regions.
summary: A new Coaches Development Program for grassroots level badminton is set to commence in Guwahati and Raipur, running from June 12th to 21st, focusing on nurturing future talent.

title: Indian Shuttlers' FIFA World Cup 2026 Picks
indian_angle: This fun feature reveals which countries top Indian badminton players like Chirag, Prannoy, Ayush, and Sindhu are rooting for in the FIFA World Cup.
summary: Top Indian badminton players share their preferences for the FIFA World Cup 2026, with Chirag-Prannoy favoring Argentina, Ayush considering Spain, and Sindhu eyeing Portugal or Germany.

title: India Opts Out of Uganda Para Badminton International
indian_angle: This news informs Indian para-badminton enthusiasts about the national team's decision not to compete in the Uganda International event.
summary: India will not be participating in the Uganda Para Badminton International 2026, as confirmed by a recent notification from the Badminton Association of India.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System for Junior Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This rule change directly impacts the development and competitive experience of young Indian badminton players in domestic tournaments and national championships.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 categories in domestic ranking tournaments and national championships.
